title: Number of Islands
description:
Given a 2D grid of `"1"` (land) and `"0"` (water), return the number of
islands. An island is surrounded by water and formed by connecting adjacent
land horizontally or vertically.

solutions.summary: Each unvisited land cell starts an island; sink its whole region.

steps.title: A helper that sinks one island
steps.explanation: From a land cell, recursively flip it and its neighbors to water so it is not counted again.

code.python:
def num_islands(grid):
    rows, cols = len(grid), len(grid[0])
    def sink(r, c):
        if r < 0 or c < 0 or r >= rows or c >= cols or grid[r][c] != "1":
            return
        grid[r][c] = "0"
        sink(r + 1, c)
        sink(r - 1, c)
        sink(r, c + 1)
        sink(r, c - 1)
    return 0

steps.title: Count and sink each new island
steps.explanation: Scan every cell; each time you hit land, increment the count and flood-fill it away.
